titleStmt.title: Transcription and Translation of BL L.F.C. Ch. xxx.7

respStmt.resp: Exported from Models of Authority data
respStmt.name: tei_exporter

availability.p: Status unknown. Exported for research and migration workflows.

revisionDesc.change: transcription moa=616 status=live modified=2021-04-05 08:19:42.325178+00 | translation moa=617 status=live modified=2021-04-05 08:19:44.457341+00

div.p:
Alex’ . dei Gratia Rex Scott’ . Omnibus probis hominibus Tocius terre sue . Clericis et Laicis 
Salutem . Sciant presentes et futuri me concessisse . et hac Carta mea confirmasse Deo . Et
Ecclesie Sancte Marie de Melros . et Monachis . ibidem deo seruientibus totam piscariam de
veteri Rokesburg . in Twede usque ad Brockestrem . quam Rex dauid . proauus meus eis
dedit . et Carta sua confirmauit . Tenendam . Sibi in liberam et perpetuam elemosinam .
Ita libere . et quiete . sicut alias elemosinas suas . liberius . et quietius tenent . et sicut
Carta Regis . dauid . proaui mei . et confirmatio domini Regis . Willelmi patris mei testantur et
confirmant . Testibus . Willelmo de Boscho . Cancellario . meo . Comite Malcolmo . de Fif’ . Philippo .
de Valoniis . Camerario . meo . Willelmo de Valoniis . filio . eius . Iohanne de Maccuswel’ . Thoma .
de lundin’ . hostiario . meo dauid . Marescallo . Heruico . Marescallo . Apud Edenb’ . tercio die
Aprilis .

Alexander, by the grace of God king of the Scots, to all worthy men of his entire land, clerk and lay, greeting. Let present and future know me to have conceded, and by this my charter to have made firm, to God and to the church of St Mary at Melrose, and to the monks serving God in that place, the whole fishery in the Tweed at Old Roxburgh, as far as ‘Brokestrem’, which King David my great-grandfather gave and his own charter made firm. For them to hold in free and everlasting alms, as freely and quitly as they most freely and quitely hold their other alms, and just as the charters of King David my great-grandfather and the confirmation of the lord King William, my father, bear witness and affirm. As witnesses: William del Bois, my chancellor; Earl Malcolm of Fife; Philip de Valognes, my chamberlain; William de Valognes, his son; John of Maxwell; Thomas of Lundie, my doorward; David the marischal; Hervey the marischal. At Edinburgh on the third day of April.
